result:
I’d make it so people could fully understand the consequences of their actions—not just intellectually, but viscerally.

Not in a punishing way. More like a built-in empathy engine. If you lie, you feel what it does to trust. If you exploit someone, you feel the weight of that imbalance. If you help someone, you feel the ripple of that too. Every action would come with a clear, undeniable sense of how it affects others and the broader system.

A lot of what goes wrong in the world isn’t because people wake up wanting to cause harm—it’s distance. Emotional distance, social distance, time-delayed consequences. Close that gap, and you don’t need as many rules, punishments, or forced morality. People would course-correct on their own because the feedback loop would be immediate and real.

It wouldn’t make the world perfect. People would still disagree, still compete, still mess up. But it would be a lot harder to justify cruelty, neglect, or indifference when you can actually feel what you’re doing.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
